policy: new_frontend is the non-deployable design/product source of truth for the new Valdrics frontend. Production code lives in frontend, but each new_frontend file must either be migrated with visual fidelity and real contracts, deferred behind a precise contract blocker, or rejected with evidence before deletion.

entries.decision: Migrated into the production approvals queue card using the real approval queue model and entitlement behavior.

Migrated the dashboard panel intent into production Operational Signals using real zombie, service-spend, and approval queue contracts. First-class cloud/SaaS/service inventory now belongs to the production /inventory route and /api/v1/inventory contract created in FME-007.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/routes/dashboard.load.test.ts src/routes/dashboard/dashboardOverviewModel.test.ts src/routes/dashboard/dashboard-page.svelte.test.ts

entries.decision: Migrated into the authenticated top bar actions and shell state.
evidence: browser QA authenticated shell desktop and mobile

entries.decision: Audited against the production SvelteKit type surface. The handoff FastAPI token/session globals and global Approval/PaginatedResponse declarations were rejected because production uses Supabase session locals, AppSession normalization, and route-local domain models. Production frontend/src/app.d.ts now keeps the Supabase Locals contract and explicitly types root PageData subscription/profile fields returned by +layout.server.ts.

Audited against the production SvelteKit shell. The useful handoff intent is covered by frontend/src/app.html through Valdrics application metadata, icon preloads, favicon/apple-touch assets, manifest.webmanifest, theme-color, color-scheme, and the required SvelteKit head/body placeholders. Handoff Google Fonts, inline FOUC style, inline no-js script, and inline display style were rejected because production uses strict CSP nonce mode, first-party assets, and app-shell CSS instead.
evidence: Official SvelteKit project-structure docs confirm src/app.html is the page template and %sveltekit.head%/%sveltekit.body% are the shell placeholders.
Official SvelteKit CSP docs confirm nonce/hash-managed CSP behavior; production keeps nonce mode and avoids manual inline shell code.

entries.decision: Audited against the production root layout server contract. The useful intent is covered by frontend/src/routes/+layout.server.ts and hooks.server.ts: public-path session skipping, Supabase safeGetSession, backend-validated profiles, subscription/profile hydration through edge proxy paths, AppSession normalization, and protected-path failure behavior. The handoff direct FASTAPI_URL calls, locals.token/workspaceId assumptions, forced onboarding redirect, and layout-level approvals payload were rejected because production keeps auth/session, onboarding, and approvals as explicit route/domain contracts.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/routes/layout.server.load.test.ts src/lib/routeProtection.test.ts --reporter=verbose

entries.decision: Migrated into the production authenticated shell while preserving real navigation and current Valdrics logo geometry.
evidence: browser QA logo asset /valdrics_icon.png desktop and mobile

entries.decision: Pending transactional email ownership decision.
entries.deletion_blocker: Requires identifying the production email rendering path and approval notification backend contract.

entries.decision: Migrated into a production /inventory route backed by /api/v1/inventory. Rows are derived from tenant-scoped AWS, Azure, GCP, AWS Organizations discovery, SaaS, license, platform, and hybrid connector/feed sources. The production view now follows the handoff compact inventory design with quick tabs, owner chips, dense table rows, status badges, and mobile column collapse. Handoff-only /inventory/add, /inventory/{id}, /ownership, /approvals/new, and unsupported owner workflow links were not copied because those require separate backend/product contracts.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/lib/lazyComponent.test.ts src/routes/inventory/inventory-page.svelte.test.ts src/routes/connections/connections-page.svelte.test.ts src/lib/persona.test.ts --reporter=verbose
corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run --reporter=dot

Playwright MCP rendered QA at /__capture/inventory: desktop 1440x1100 and mobile 390x844, screenshots inspected with view_image, compact header/table/owner-chip/mobile-collapse verified, quick tabs filter fixture rows, table roles exposed, expiring badge orange verified, console warnings/errors 0, document overflow 0

entries.decision: Migrated the landing visual direction into the production public landing route while preserving authenticated redirect behavior, public auth-intent links, SEO metadata, current production pricing, and the primary Valdrics logo asset at frontend/static/valdrics_icon.png. Handoff-only /login and /signup routes, $299 Starter / $799 Team pricing, 14-day trial/no-card claims, and custom SVG logo marks were rejected.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/lib/landing/landingPricingContract.test.ts src/routes/pricing/pricing-page.svelte.test.ts src/routes/pricing/pricing.load.test.ts --reporter=verbose

corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/lib/landing/landingPricingContract.test.ts src/routes/pricing/pricing-page.svelte.test.ts src/routes/pricing/pricing.load.test.ts src/routes/page.server.motion.test.ts src/lib/landing/publicNav.test.ts src/routes/layout-public-menu.svelte.test.ts --reporter=verbose

Playwright rendered QA on http://localhost:5174/: desktop 1440x1100 and mobile 390x844 pricing screenshots saved under /tmp/valdrics-landing-*-20260602-v2.png; hero logo screenshots saved under /tmp/valdrics-landing-hero-*-20260602.png and /tmp/valdrics-landing-hero-desktop-preview-wait.png; verified /valdrics_icon.png loads, Free/Starter/Growth/Pro pricing is visible, stale $799 Team/trial/no-card claims absent, legacy /login and /signup absent, console errors 0, horizontal overflow 0

entries.decision: Migrated the useful dark auth-card visual treatment into the production /auth/login route while preserving the current Supabase /auth/flow, /auth/callback, /auth/session, public auth intent, and same-origin redirect contracts. Handoff-only direct FASTAPI_URL actions, route-group /login path, Svelte transition directives, custom SVG logo mark, inline-style risk, forgot-password link, and server-cookie assumptions were rejected.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/routes/auth/login/login-page.svelte.test.ts src/routes/auth/flow/flow-route.server.test.ts src/routes/auth/callback/callback-route.server.test.ts src/routes/auth/session/session-route.server.test.ts src/routes/auth/logout/logout-page.server.test.ts
Browser QA /auth/login?intent=roi_assessment&persona=cfo desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844: console errors 0, horizontal overflow false, current valdrics_icon.png loaded, signup controls visible.

entries.decision: Migrated the onboarding wizard visual intent into the production provider setup flow while preserving the existing AWS, Azure, GCP, SaaS, License/ITAM, discovery, tier-gate, and connector validation contracts. Handoff-only SaaS tools, policy defaults, team invite, and complete-workspace actions were not copied because they are not backed by the current onboarding route contract.

entries.decision: Extracted the focused wizard shell, progress rail, provider card density, discovery panel, and success-state styling into the production onboarding route. Static HTML-only state, hand-rolled store assumptions, and backend endpoint guesses were rejected in favor of the existing SvelteKit route modules and tested onboarding API helpers.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/routes/onboarding/onboarding-page.svelte.test.ts

entries.decision: Migrated the useful ownership-routing intent into production Identity settings using the real /api/v1/settings/identity SSO, federation, and SCIM group-mapping contract. The handoff standalone /ownership route, direct FASTAPI_URL browser calls, /ownership/resources, /teams, /ownership/stats, /ownership/assign endpoints, synthetic owner tables, and fake resource coverage counters were rejected because production resource-owner assignment needs a dedicated backend contract before it becomes UI.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/lib/components/IdentitySettingsCard.svelte.test.ts

entries.decision: Rejected as a deployable dependency manifest. The production dependency graph is governed by frontend/package.json and frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml, which already pin the active SvelteKit 2, Svelte 5, Vite 7, Cloudflare adapter, Playwright, Vitest, Supabase, Tailwind, and lucide stack. Handoff-only Slack, Resend, MJML, Supabase CLI, and ESLint manifest entries are not production frontend requirements until a real route/backend contract needs them.
evidence: Compared dependency manifests: handoff-only runtime deps were @slack/web-api, mjml, and resend; production-only/current deps include @sveltejs/kit 2.57.1, svelte 5.53.0, vite 7.3.2, @sveltejs/adapter-cloudflare 7.2.8, @playwright/test 1.58.0, vitest 4.0.16, typescript 5.9.3, @supabase/supabase-js 2.90.1, tailwindcss 4.1.18, @tailwindcss/postcss 4.1.18, @lucide/svelte 0.562.0, and dompurify 3.3.1

entries.decision: Migrated the useful policy-engine visual intent into the production /settings enforcement control plane. Production keeps the existing edge-proxy /enforcement/policies, /enforcement/budgets, and /enforcement/credits contracts, Pro entitlement gating, and route-local settings architecture. Handoff-only /policies, /policies/new, /policies/{id}/edit, direct FASTAPI_URL calls, locals.token assumptions, unsupported trigger counters, and copied policy catalog records were rejected because they are not backed by the current production contract.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/lib/components/enforcementSettingsModel.test.ts src/lib/components/EnforcementSettingsCard.svelte.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=20000
Local route/API audit confirmed there is no production /policies route; the real policy surface is /settings via edge-proxy /enforcement/* endpoints

entries.decision: Migrated the savings page visual intent into the production Savings Proof route using current /savings/proof, /savings/proof/drilldown, and /savings/realized/events contracts. Handoff-only /savings/summary, /savings/events, saving type, team name, event description, and annual-total fields were rejected because they are not backed by the current backend contract.
evidence: corepack pnpm@10.32.1 --dir frontend exec vitest run src/routes/savings/savings-page.svelte.test.ts

entries.decision: Migrated the useful signup-mode visual intent into the production /auth/login?mode=signup flow instead of creating unsupported /signup compatibility. Production keeps current auth intent links, callback redirects, pricing plan intent, and Supabase signup/magic-link behavior. Handoff-only 14-day trial/no-credit-card copy, direct register endpoint, full-name/workspace/password-confirm fields, password-strength inline styles, emoji reveal control, target=_blank legal links without rel, and /login link were rejected because they are not backed by current production auth/pricing contracts.
